title: Change Manifest
description: One file per unit of work at .spec/changes/<slug>.json. A change is the primary unit of work; areas/contracts remain the logical spec boundary. The manifest references targets and tracks per-target workflow state (checked/applied/verified) — it never duplicates spec content, only IDs. Committed with the PR; status 'landed' on merge makes it a changelog entry. Deleting every manifest leaves the specs complete.

change.description: Slug. Doubles as the suggested branch name (change/<slug>) and commit scope (spec(<slug>): ...).

intent.description: One sentence: what this change accomplishes, in domain terms.

status.description: open = created, no spec edits yet; in-progress = edits underway; landed = PR merged; abandoned = dropped without landing.

branch.description: Git branch carrying the change, if one was created.

targets.description: Every area/contract/ui spec this change touches. Contracts spanning a touched area are auto-added (auto: true).

auto.description: true when pulled in automatically (contract cascade) rather than edited explicitly.

ids.description: REQ/INV/PROP/CON/DEC/Q IDs added or modified in this target by this change. References only — content lives in the area JSON.

checked.description: Last /spec-check on this target (at current spec state) passed.

applied.description: /spec-apply has generated/updated code for this change's IDs. N/A for contracts.

verified.description: Last /spec-verify on this target passed. N/A for contracts.

open_questions.description: Cross-target question refs in <area>/Q-NNN form, blocking this change.
